text:
BOTTOM LINE — Energy surge, tech wreck. SPY -0.77% and QQQ -1.90% fell as oil +8.36% crushed semis -4.77%, creating a violent rotation away from tech into energy despite a broad market selloff.

INDICES — VIX 17.2 elevated above its 20-day MA. Rank by return: IWM -0.85%, SPY -0.77%, DIA -0.25%, QQQ -1.90%, SOXX -4.77%. Small > large by 0.38pp. Oil +8.36% was the standout cross-asset mover. QQQ is trading in no-man's land relative to its 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.8x with price down = low-conviction distribution; QQQ vol_ratio 0.9x with price down = moderate distribution. XLE volume spiked 1.3x as energy rallied, signaling real accumulation in that sector.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +3.01%, XLU Utilities +0.68%, XLF Financials +0.65%, XLRE Real Estate +0.56%, XLP Staples +0.56%, XLV Healthcare +0.35%, XLC Comms -0.04%, XLB Materials -0.61%, XLI Industrials -0.85%, XLY Discretionary -1.02%, XLK Technology -2.42%. Top Industries: USO +8.36%, XOP +4.17%. Bottom: SOXX -4.77%, DRAM -9.11%. Money flowed into energy and utilities, out of tech and semiconductors — a clear 'energy rotation' day.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 2.43 and QQQ 1.55 signal heavy protection demand. VIX 17.2 above its 20-day MA of 17.0 confirms elevated fear. Skew positive on SPY (+6.3%) and QQQ (+15.2%) shows put premium priced in.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +8.36% versus SOXX -4.77% is the day's primary transmission: crude rally hammering tech growth assumptions. Gold -2.62% fell alongside risk assets despite VIX up, a divergence, likely due to dollar +0.39% strength.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Greed score 63.1/100. Top movers: USO* +8.36% (energy), SOXX -4.77% (semis), DRAM -9.11% (memory). 20d momentum extremes: best sector Energy +3.01%, worst sector Tech -2.42%. Vol spike ticker USO at 2.3x avg vol with price up = accumulation in energy. Watch tomorrow: SOXX — -4.77% near gamma flip, semis at risk of further dealer-driven selling; XLE — +3.01% on 1.3x volume, momentum breakout potential; QQQ — -1.90% with put-call ratio 1.55, heavy protection may cap downside.
